subject: Re: Warp on Linux installation issue
body:
Also thanks for Ben's suggestions. I have installed warp with conda anyway.
Maybe other guys can try it.

Best

On Tue, Nov 26, 2024 at 12:43 AM Benjamin Barad <benjamin.barad@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Not trying to be difficult, but heads up that mambaforge is deprecated,
> and miniforge3 is what you want now!
> https://conda-forge.org/news/2024/07/29/sunsetting-mambaforge/
>
> On Mon, Nov 25, 2024 at 6:57 AM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Yunjie,
>>
>> Problems solving environments typically happen when people install a
>> bunch of things into their base environment. You might also be using the
>> conda solver rather than mamba, conda can be slow. I would recommend nuking
>> your conda installation and starting again with the latest version of
>> mambaforge :-)
>>
>> Miniforge Releases <https://conda-forge.org/miniforge/>
>> conda-forge.org <https://conda-forge.org/miniforge/>
>> <https://conda-forge.org/miniforge/> <https://conda-forge.org/miniforge/>
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>
>> On Nov 24, 2024, at 21:42, Yunjie Chang <changyj0821@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿Hi warp list,
>>
>> I am trying to install warp on my linux workstation (Ubuntu 22.04, nvidia
>> 4090). Using this command:
>> conda create -n warp warp -c warpem -c nvidia/label/cuda-11.8.0 -c
>> pytorch -c conda-forge
>>
>> However, after I hit enter in the terminal, it is just keep "Solving
>> environment". I have waited for hours and nothing changed.
>>
>> Any idea to solve this issue?
>>
>> Best
>> Yunjie
>>
>> --
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups
>> "Warp" group.

Best
Yunjie Chang
